is a [Canadian](/wiki/Canadians "Canadians") politician who was elected to represent the [riding](/wiki/Electoral_district_(Canada) "Electoral district (Canada)") of [Moose Jaw—Lake Centre—Lanigan](/wiki/Moose_Jaw%E2%80%94Lake_Centre%E2%80%94Lanigan "Moose Jaw—Lake Centre—Lanigan") in the [House of Commons of Canada](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_Canada "House of Commons of Canada") in the [2021 Canadian federal election](/wiki/2021_Canadian_federal_election "2021 Canadian federal election").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

["Elections Canada officially declares Fraser Tolmie winner during 2021 federal election"](https://www.moosejawtoday.com/local-news/elections-canada-officially-declares-fraser-tolmie-winner-during-2021-federal-election-4354228). *Jason G. Antonio*. Moose Jaw Today. Retrieved 22 September 2021.
["About Fraser - Fraser Tolmie, MP"](https://frasertolmiemp.ca/about/). *Fraser Tolmie*. Retrieved 19 December 2023.

Canadian politician

**Fraser Tolmie** is a [Canadian](/wiki/Canadians "Canadians") politician who was elected to represent the [riding](/wiki/Electoral_district_(Canada) "Electoral district (Canada)") of [Moose Jaw—Lake Centre—Lanigan](/wiki/Moose_Jaw%E2%80%94Lake_Centre%E2%80%94Lanigan "Moose Jaw—Lake Centre—Lanigan") in the [House of Commons of Canada](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_Canada "House of Commons of Canada") in the [2021 Canadian federal election](/wiki/2021_Canadian_federal_election "2021 Canadian federal election").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fraser_Tolmie&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Prior to his election to Parliament, Tolmie served as mayor of [Moose Jaw](/wiki/Moose_Jaw "Moose Jaw"), [Saskatchewan](/wiki/Saskatchewan "Saskatchewan") from 2016 to 2021. He was as a city councillor of Moose Jaw from 2009 to 2012.

Tolmie was an officer with the [Royal Canadian Air Force](/wiki/Royal_Canadian_Air_Force "Royal Canadian Air Force").

He obtained a degree in War Studies/Strategic Thought and Conflict Resolution at [King's College London](/wiki/King%27s_College_London "King's College London").[[2]](#cite_note-2)
